minutes_briefs.title: Emergency management reports must now be published; personal information can be shared with US officials
minutes_briefs.summary: New BC regulations under the Emergency and Disaster Management Act require the provincial administrator to publish reports about emergencies and disasters. These reports come from local authorities or from the Nisga'a Nation or a treaty first nation (if they ask for it to be published). The rules also allow personal information to be shared with a wider group of people, including elected officials, employees, or agents of other Canadian provinces, the US government, or any US state, as well as anyone who has a signed agreement with the minister. This means your personal details could be given to US authorities during an emergency.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required, but review your privacy policies and emergency plans to understand how your personal or customer information may be shared.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 79/2026
Made under: Animal Health Act
Published in: No. 9 – B.C. Regs. 77 to 82, May 19, 2026
Deposit date: May 11, 2026

B.C. Reg. 79/2026, deposited May 11, 2026, under theANIMAL HEALTH ACT[sections 96 (3) and 98],EMERGENCY AND DISASTER MANAGEMENT ACT[sections 164, 166, 167, 172, 173 and 209],STRATA PROPERTY ACT[section 292 (2)] andWATER SUSTAINABILITY ACT[section 127 (3)]. Order in Council 186/2026, approved and ordered May 11, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders the following: (a)	section 202 of theEmergency and Disaster Management Act, S.B.C. 2023, c. 37, is brought into force; (b)	the Emergency and Disaster Management Regulation, B.C. Reg. 235/2023, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule 1; (c)	the regulations listed in the attached Schedule 2 are amended as set out in that Schedule; (i)	section 47 of theEmergency and Disaster Management Act, S.B.C. 2023, c. 37, is brought into force, and (ii)	the Emergency and Disaster Management Regulation, B.C. Reg. 235/2023, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule 3. — K. GREENE,Minister of Emergency Management and Climate Readiness; J. SUNNER,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	Section 6.1 of the Emergency and Disaster Management Regulation, B.C. Reg. 235/2023, is amended by adding the following subsection: (3)	The provincial administrator must publish the following: (a)	a report provided to the provincial administrator under section 121 (2) of the Act; (b)	a report prepared by the Nisga’a Nation or a treaty first nation under section 121 (4) of the Act, if the Nisga’a Nation or the treaty first nation requests publication of the report. 2	The following section is added to Division 1: Disclosure of personal information 7.1For the purposes of section 159 (2) (b) of the Act, the following classes of persons are prescribed: (a)	an elected or appointed official, an employee or an agent of any of the following: (ii)	the government of a province or another jurisdiction in Canada; (iii)	the government of the United States; (iv)	the government of a state of, or another jurisdiction in, the United States; (d)	a person who has entered into an agreement with the minister under section 8 of the Act;
